homepage.heroHeadline: Lawn Care Tailored to North Carolina's Climate
homepage.heroSubline: Expert care for Raleigh's unique soil and weather challenges. Serving Durham, Cary, and Chapel Hill with science-backed lawn solutions.
aboutText: Founded by a NC State horticulture graduate, Horizon Lawn Care combines academic expertise with hands-on experience maintaining Triangle-area lawns.
We adapt our methods to Piedmont clay soil, using drought-resistant techniques in summer and preventative winterization for Raleigh's occasional ice storms.
Our team lives in the communities we serve, from Downtown Raleigh's historic districts to North Hills' new developments.

homepage.ctaSubline: Free analysis of your lawn's unique challenges in Wake County

services.name: Clay Soil Solutions
services.shortDescription: Transform Raleigh's stubborn red clay into fertile lawn-ready soil.
services.description:
Piedmont clay dominates lawns from Downtown Raleigh to Cary, creating drainage issues that drown grass roots in summer rains and bake hard in drought. Our 3-step process begins with core aeration to relieve compaction, followed by strategic compost incorporation to improve texture. We finish with pH-balanced gypsum treatments that help particles aggregate without harming nearby oak trees.

For new constructions in North Hills or Apex where builders scrape away topsoil, we install 4" of quality screened loam before seeding. All amendments use organic materials from NC suppliers, avoiding synthetic polymers that fail in our freeze-thaw cycles.
process: Step 1: Soil test identifies pH and compaction levels
Step 2: Deep-tine aeration with 3" hollow tines
Step 3: Custom blend of compost and mineral amendments
Step 4: Overseeding with drought-tolerant fescue blends
benefits: Eliminate standing water after summer storms
Reduce required watering by up to 40%
Create habitat for beneficial soil microbes

faq.question: How often does Raleigh clay need amending?
faq.answer: Most lawns need annual aeration and biennial compost topdressing. New constructions often require 3 years of intensive remediation.

faq.question: Will this help with my Japanese maple's yellow leaves?
faq.answer: Yes - we address iron and manganese deficiencies common in Wake County's alkaline clay that affect ornamentals.

services.name: Bermuda & Zoysia Care
services.shortDescription: Specialized programs for Raleigh's most common warm-season grasses.
services.description:
Bermuda grass thrives in Raleigh's summer heat but requires precise timing for spring green-up and fall dormancy. Our monthly service includes targeted pre-emergent applications timed to NC State's growing degree day models, preventing crabgrass without harming your turf. For zoysia lawns common in Morrisville's newer developments, we use low-nitrogen fertilizers that prevent thatch buildup while encouraging deep roots.

Unlike generic national programs, we adjust mowing heights seasonally - cutting Bermuda at 1.5" during peak growth but raising to 2" during August droughts. All clippings are processed through our specialized mowers that mulch finely enough to decompose rapidly, eliminating the need for bagging in most cases.
process: Step 1: Dormant pre-emergent application in early March
Step 2: First mow at 50% green-up with sharpened reels
Step 3: Monthly liquid fertilizers with micronutrients
Step 4: Fall potassium boost for winter hardiness
benefits: Reduce water needs with deeper roots
Maintain green color longer in fall

faq.question: Why does my Bermuda thin out near pine trees?
faq.answer: Pine straw raises soil acidity - we apply targeted lime treatments and switch to shade-tolerant varieties in these zones.

faq.question: When should I transition from Bermuda to fescue?
faq.answer: We recommend cool-season blends only for shaded ITB properties - Bermuda outperforms in 90% of Raleigh lawns.

services.name: Storm Damage Cleanup
services.shortDescription: Rapid response for fallen limbs and flood-damaged lawns.
services.description:
Raleigh's severe summer thunderstorms and occasional ice storms leave lawns vulnerable. Our emergency crews prioritize safety with certified arborists for hanging limb removal in neighborhoods like Five Points with mature hardwoods. For flood-damaged areas near Crabtree Creek, we employ specialized hydroseeding to rebuild washed-out topsoil without importing weed seeds.

After major events like 2023's Christmas ice storm, we implement a 3-phase recovery: immediate debris removal with brush hogs, soil aeration to relieve compaction from equipment traffic, and finally customized reseeding based on sun exposure. All work is documented for insurance claims with before/after photos geotagged to your property.
process: Step 1: Safety inspection and hazard mitigation
Step 2: Selective debris removal preserving healthy plants
Step 3: Soil remediation based on flood duration
Step 4: Erosion control matting on steep banks

faq.question: Will you remove a tree that fell on my fence?
faq.answer: We handle all lawn-related debris and can recommend licensed arborists for structural damage.

faq.question: How soon after flooding can you reseed?
faq.answer: After waters recede, we wait 48 hours for soil to drain before assessing - rushing can wash away new seed.

services.name: New Home Lawn Installation
services.shortDescription: From builder's clay to established lawn in one season.
services.description:
New developments in Apex and Brier Creek often leave homeowners staring at compacted subsoil dotted with construction debris. Our turnkey process begins with thorough soil screening to remove nails and concrete chunks, followed by subsoiling to break up plow pans. We then grade properties to direct water away from foundations - critical in Raleigh's heavy downpours.

For quick establishment, we install certified TifTuf Bermuda sod on sunny lots and GeoZoysia on partial shade properties. Both varieties were tested at NC State for our climate. Irrigation systems are carefully programmed to phase out supplemental watering as roots establish, preventing the overwatering that causes fungal issues in new lawns.
process: Step 1: Debris removal and subsoil decompaction
Step 2: Precision grading with laser levels
Step 3: Organic matter incorporation
Step 4: Sod installation with staggered seams

faq.question: How long until my new sod can handle foot traffic?
faq.answer: Light use after 2 weeks, full use at 4 weeks - we'll provide specific guidance based on weather.

faq.question: Do you work with my builder's landscaper?
faq.answer: Yes - we coordinate with all parties to meet certificate of occupancy requirements.

services.name: Native Plant Landscaping
services.shortDescription: Low-maintenance, drought-resistant gardens that support local ecology.
services.description:
Chapel Hill and Durham homeowners increasingly seek landscapes that support bees, butterflies, and hummingbirds. Our designs use NC-native plants like purple coneflower and beardtongue that thrive in Piedmont clay with minimal irrigation. We cluster plants by water needs, creating micro-zones that prevent overwatering drought-tolerant species.

For properties near the Museum Park or Umstead Park, we incorporate deer-resistant varieties and strategic brush piles that shelter songbirds. Maintenance programs focus on selective hand-weeding to preserve self-seeding annuals, with organic pest controls that won't harm pollinators. All designs include winter interest elements like evergreen mountain mint and berry-producing shrubs.
process: Step 1: Site analysis of sun patterns and drainage
Step 2: Custom plant palette selection
Step 3: Soil preparation with mycorrhizal inoculants
Step 4: Installation with proper spacing for maturity

faq.question: Will native plants survive under my oak trees?
faq.answer: Yes - we use understory species like wild ginger and Christmas fern adapted to dry shade.

faq.question: How do I prevent rabbits from eating everything?
faq.answer: We install discreet rabbit fencing for vulnerable young plants until established.

reviews.name: Meredith K.
reviews.text: After years of failed DIY attempts to fix the swampy mess behind our 1960s ranch near Pullen Park, Horizon's core aeration and compost topdressing solved it in one season. They even identified an underground spring contributing to the issue.

reviews.name: Devin R.
reviews.text: Our Brier Creek builder left us with nothing but compacted clay. Horizon screened out construction debris, graded properly, and installed zoysia that stayed green through last summer's drought. Neighbors are jealous.

reviews.name: Lisa T.
reviews.text: When the ice storm took down three dogwoods onto our Five Points lawn, they had crews out within 12 hours. The careful root zone protection during cleanup saved our remaining trees.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: As a transplant from Michigan, I didn't know how to care for Bermuda grass. Horizon's monthly service has our North Hills lawn looking better than the golf courses at Prestonwood.

reviews.name: Naomi W.
reviews.text: Our Chapel Hill cottage garden is now a Certified Wildlife Habitat thanks to Horizon's native plant design. The monarch butterflies arrived within two weeks of planting!

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.text: They transformed our brick-hard Downtown Raleigh yard into actual soil that grows grass. Only complaint is they're so popular it took 3 weeks to get on their schedule.

serviceAreas.content:
Horizon Lawn Care proudly serves the vibrant city of Durham, NC, where historic charm meets modern living. Our expert lawn care services are tailored to the unique needs of Durham's diverse landscapes, from renovated industrial lofts downtown to sprawling suburban yards. With hot, humid summers, maintaining a healthy lawn requires specialized care, and our team is equipped to handle everything from fertilization to weed control.

Durham's mix of urban and suburban environments means we encounter a variety of lawn care challenges. Whether you're in a historic neighborhood or a newer development, we provide customized solutions to keep your lawn lush and green year-round. Our services include mowing, aeration, and seasonal clean-ups, ensuring your outdoor space thrives in Durham's climate.
serviceAreas.localContext: Durham is known for its renovated industrial lofts downtown and a thriving cultural scene.

serviceAreas.content:
Horizon Lawn Care is your trusted partner for maintaining beautiful lawns in Cary, NC. This family-friendly community features master-planned neighborhoods from the 1980s to 2000s, each with its own landscaping needs. Our team understands the importance of curb appeal in Cary's well-kept communities and offers comprehensive lawn care services to match.

Cary's hot, humid summers can be tough on grass, but our tailored fertilization and pest control programs ensure your lawn stays healthy. From regular mowing to seasonal landscaping, we help homeowners achieve the pristine yards that Cary is known for. Let us take the hassle out of lawn care so you can enjoy your outdoor space.
serviceAreas.localContext: Cary is characterized by its master-planned communities and family-oriented atmosphere.
